title: Dasystephana frigida
intro: Dasystephana frigida, commonly known as the cold Dasystephana, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, contributing to its potential in relieving pain and reducing oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Dasystephana frigida include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to possess antimicrobial and antiviral activities. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Dasystephana frigida is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Dasystephana, which is a group of plants commonly known as the 'butterfly bushes'.
